eve: noun. The day or night before, usually used for holidays, such as Christmas Eve.
ewe: noun. A female sheep, as opposed to a ram.
ewer: noun. A kind of widemouthed pitcher or jug with a shape like a vase and a handle, and originally used for carrying water.
ore: noun. Rock or other material that contains valuable or utilitarian materials; primarily a rock containing metals or gems for which it is
overview: noun. A brief summary, as of a book or a presentation.
owe: verb. To be under an obligation to give something back to someone or to perform some action for someone.
ree: noun. Alternative form of rei (“Portuguese real”).
rev: verb. To increase the speed of a motor, or to operate at a higher speed.
review: noun. A second or subsequent reading of a text or artifact in an attempt to gain new insights.
revive: verb. To cause (a person or animal) to recover from a faint; to cause (a person or animal) to return to a state of consciousness.
rive: verb. To tear apart by force; to rend; to split; to cleave.
row: noun. A line of objects, often regularly spaced, such as seats in a theatre, vegetable plants in a garden, etc.
vee: noun. The name of the Latin script letter V / v.
veer: verb. To let out (a sail-line), to allow (a sheet) to run out.
verve: noun. Enthusiasm, rapture, spirit, or vigour, especially of imagination such as that which animates an artist, musician, or writer, in
vie: verb. To fight for superiority; to contend; to compete eagerly so as to gain something.
vier: noun. Spoken by a player during a match with one or more visually impaired players to indicate the fourth rank in algebraic notation.
viewer: noun. Someone who views a spectacle; an onlooker or spectator.
vireo: noun. Any of a number of small insectivorous passerine birds, of the genus Vireo, that have grey-green plumage.
voe: noun. A narrow sea inlet, particularly in the Shetland Islands, similar to a fjord.
vow: noun. A solemn promise to perform some act, or behave in a specified manner, especially a promise to live and act in accordance with the
vower: noun. (Can we clean up this sense?) One who makes a vow.
vrow: noun. Alternative form of vrouw (“a Dutchwoman”).
weir: noun. An adjustable dam placed across a river to regulate the flow of water downstream.
were: verb. second-person singular simple past indicative of be
wire: noun. Metal formed into a thin, even thread, now usually by being drawn through a hole in a steel die.
woe: noun. Great sadness or distress; a misfortune causing such sadness.
eir: determiner. Belonging to em, gender-neutral third-person singular possessive adjective, equivalent to the singular their and coordinate
evoe: interjection. Alternative form of euoi (“Bacchic cry”).
iwo: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Iwur.
oie: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Okolie.
ower: noun. A person who owes something, especially money.
revie: verb. To vie with, or rival, in return.
rew: noun. Obsolete or dialectal form of row
rio: noun. Alternative form of ryō (“Japanese ounce”).
wer: noun. A man; a male; a husband.
wir: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Wiraféd.
